blocks.content: Among the refugees aboard the three hundred ships, confusion was evident on their faces.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Did we survive?
No one had expected to live when the drones attacked, but a bizarre phenomenon had occurred.
A spatial barrier fluctuated and suddenly thousands of bombs had all detonated.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Trying it out for real turned out to be quite tricky.
Slate of Hand was not magic, but rather a trick-like skill.
This trick occurred at the quantum level, seemingly disregarding even the laws of physics.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: Such a method...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If a magician deceives the eyes, Shirone deceives cognition.
At the moment when everyone in the audience is certain of the future, the mind becomes something predictable, hence a law.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: And if it's a law, Shirone could twist the event they perceived.
But the reason it's a trick... is because once people's perception returns, the event itself regains its logical probability.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Heh.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If someone had doubted a different future, the three hundred ships would never have disappeared.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A magic trick with 20,000 lives at stake. It was a moment of artistry, brought about by focusing everyone's perception squarely in the blind spot.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Certainly not sane, that.
With that said.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: What will you do? Are you going to die at my hand, or wait for the Messiah to come?
As Poine glanced over at the pirate ship, the face of Jacra on deck was distorted.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: Hmm.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Captain, what should we do?

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: If the opponent was a first-class dragon, pretending to deliberate seemed the human thing to do.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Is there a need to wait?
Electricity coursed through Jacra's eyes, and a powerful magnetic field encased him like a sphere.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Let's go, boys!
The crew, realizing his sincerity, activated the machinery, and the pirate ship began to turn transparent.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Stealth? Pathetic. A high-level technique, but nothing a dragon couldn't handle.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: Raaah!
Poine tucked her wings and dove as only a blurry outline of the pirate ship remained.
Pfoo!
As a drenched Poine shook off the water and scanned the surroundings, a horn sounded in the distance.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Kahahaha! Here, here!

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Come at me, lizard.
Jacra, standing on the deck, provocatively flicked his hand and raised his middle finger.
Rrrrr...
It was technology handling energy at a level never achieved by current humanity...
Raaah!
Already enraged, Poine surged upwards, casting magic circles all around.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Dissolve.
From the magic circle created by the dragon's tongue, highly toxic acid sprayed out under immense pressure.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Puhahaha! See that? The lizard's lost it!
The pirate ship, maneuvering through teleportation powered by electric energy, was agile regardless of size and mass.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Captain! Over there…

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Enough! Fire away! Full output for the energy guns! Today's a lizard party!

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: No, that's not it!
The crew, holding their noses, shouted.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: The ship is melting!
Narrowing his eyes, Jacra surveyed the surroundings as the nearby sea turned green.
Hundreds of thousands of fish floated up, resembling a land covered in corpses.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: ...What about Langi?
Jacra turned towards the Arachne sailing ship and pressed his temple, pulling the view closer.
The knights and pirates were deep in battle.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Kahaha! Too easy!
The stealth-armed pirates were slashing the knights' necks from unseen angles.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Damn it! I need to see to fight!
The knights, retreating continuously, were pushed back to the entrance of the inner chamber guarded by Cairns.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Idiots! And you call yourselves knights of Arachne!
Once the enemies entered the inner chamber, looting the gold and treasures inside was just a matter of time.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: No, more importantly, Langi...
Recalling the single tribute King Dionas of Tharsis desired, he suddenly realized.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Where did she go?
Langi, who had been on deck until just a while ago aboard Shirone's ship, was nowhere to be seen.
Heugh! Heugh!
Running down the corridor of the inner chamber lined with rooms, Langi looked back.
Huh!
Realizing something ghostly was still trailing her, a chill ran down her spine.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: The room won't work. It's a dead end. But even running along the corridor would only mean being trapped on a ship in the vast ocean.
Kyah!
As Langi turned her head, she felt a blunt impact on her face and fell to the ground.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Are you Langi?
Startled, she looked up as Jacra, having deactivated stealth mode, appeared.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: He'll like you.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: Please, save me.
Although Langi was used to dealing with villains, the look in Jacra's eyes was not normal.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Don't even beg... Just come nicely, and nothing bad will happen. Got it?
Langi nodded.
Hmm.
Watching her desperate expression, Jacra grabbed her wrist and opened a room door.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Can't help it. Let's talk first.
Kyaa!
Langi struggled, but could not overcome Jacra's strong grasp.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Keke, what a windfall today...
As Jacra opened the room door, a swirl of light began to condense rapidly in front of him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Photon Cannon.
As Shirone, who had arrived by quantum transmission, cast a spell, Jacra was blown backwards.
As her wrist was released, Langi, clutching her head, collapsed and looked back.
A trail where a person had been blown through the wall stretched out before her.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ms. Langi, are you alright?
Turning her head at the voice, Shirone extended a worried hand.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: How, how could this be?
In a sealed room, it was impossible for Shirone to appear.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: First, get up. I'll take you to a safe place.
As Langi took his hand, Jacra's roaring voice echoed through the hole in the wall.
That damn...!
Close by, the voice continued.
Son of a!
Jacra appeared in front of them, swinging his fist, but Shirone pulled Langi back.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A space jump ability. Close to a mix of Flicker and Spark, but the issue wasn't magic.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: An artifact of Maika?
Jacra raised an eyebrow.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Keke, want to know? If you want to know, hand over the girl.
No fool would negotiate with a pirate.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Dislike it? Really dislike it? Then...
Pressing his temple, Jacra's body became transparent and quickly disappeared.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sh, Shirone.
As Langi clung to Shirone's arm, voices echoed from all around.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: How about it? Don't know where I am? Shall I cut your throat or the woman's wrist?
Shirone watched ahead intensely.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How about smashing the ship? If we pile up treasures under the sea, it might attract some attention.
The voice was a decoy; Jacra, with his mouth tightly shut, was behind Shirone, drawing his sword.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Die.
At that moment, Jacra's eyes widened.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: What is this?
In his vision connected to his brain, Shirone's figure appeared to vibrate into multiple images.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Random numbers counted down rapidly, and undecodable ancient characters flickered.
-Formulation level, electronic grade.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: What do I care.
As the blade aimed for the crown of the head, Shirone twisted his waist.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Dodged?
Technically, it was a matter of probability.
Photon Cannon in hand, Shirone, turning with the momentum, crashed into Jacra's abdomen.
Kugh!
A magnetic field shield unfolded, and again, indecipherable characters rose.
-Defense impossible. Exceeds electromagnetic field tolerance limit.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Damn it! What the hell...!
Again sent flying through the opposite wall, Jacra, frustrated, cursed loudly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What are you babbling about!
With a thud as the last wall broke, the cold sea water engulfed his body.
Like a squall, the cool sound of water rushing in, Shirone led Langi.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let's go. The outer wall is breached.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: What about the treasures? There are over 20 tons loaded here.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's not ours anyway. Why, do you care?
...Yes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Act quickly, and we might prevent capsizing. First, we need to catch Jacra.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Maika's artifact.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's a civilization that uses electricity. There's a good chance it's connected to the outside world.
As Langi took his hand and they moved to the deck, most of the knights were dead.
Cairns appeared, radiant.

blocks.speaker: Cairns
blocks.content: Star of the Ivory Tower! Save us...
Before he could finish, Shirone cast a wide spell, hurling the pirates off the ship.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Where's Jacra?
In the distance, Poine was sinking most of the pirate ship.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Nowhere to return to. And the vast ocean.
If it were a space jump of that magnitude, Langi would no longer be here.
As if answering the question, screams came from the direction of the ship where Shirone was staying.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Save me! Save me!
A wet Jacra, holding a knife to a middle-aged woman's throat, staggered.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Ugh, annoying. Damn machine.
Attempting a space jump, only sparks erupted in his mind without response.
He tried to space jump, but only sparks burst in his mind without any reaction.
Shirone soared into the sky.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Give up. If you surrender now, I'll ensure you face justice through the proper legal process.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: You're talking nonsense.
Dragging the woman to the edge of the deck, Jacra looked towards the sea.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: There's no poison here.
With his specialty in diving, he could easily escape this predicament.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Don't move, anyone. If you chase me, I'll slit this woman's throat. Got it?
Jacra grinned confidently.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Ha, this is too easy. Human lives are as fragile as leaves. How many hostages are there anyway?
Stealthily, he grabbed the woman and jumped into the sea.
Goodbye!
Even as he splashed down, Shirone did not move.
Blub! Blub!
The woman struggled in the water, but Jacra swam calmly.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Diving record: 23 minutes.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: His ability to surpass human limits in holding his breath was because he was a Schema morpher.
Most people can't last even three minutes. The same for this woman. But in those three minutes...

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: I can escape from Shirone.
If I hide in the deep sea, what can they possibly do? Please last long enough. Let me live.
Blub! Blub!
The sounds of bubbles popping from the woman's throat were music to Jacra's ears.
Huff! Huff!
As the woman took a deep breath and screamed.

blocks.speaker: Jacra
blocks.content: Save me!
Ha! Idiot! This is the sea...
Only then did Jacra realize something was odd as he looked around to see a wall of water had formed.
What? What?
In the middle of a 200-meter diameter, the sea level had dropped, reaching the ocean floor.
The sea wall bulged out and then a translucent hand formed, approaching.
Hand of God—Elemental Hand.
The hand, like an aquarium allowing deep-sea fish to swim within its grasp, pulled the woman up to the surface.
Shirone approached, hands behind his back.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Any last words?
Uh, well, um...
Still in disbelief, Jacra raised his hands and said, Surrender?
A massive amount of water then crashed down, smashing him into the seabed.
